title: centaurea austrobalcanica subsp. austrobalcanica
intro: Centaurea austrobalcanica subsp. austrobalcanica, also known as Balkan knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to the Balkan Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Centaurea austrobalcanica subsp. austrobalcanica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
